pattern: Flipping every bit of a number is the same as XOR-ing it with the all-ones mask that is exactly as wide as the number's own binary length.
hints: Flipping each bit b is the operation 1 - b, which for a whole number is XOR against a string of ones the same width as the value.
You only need a mask of ones up to the highest set bit; counting how many bits the number occupies tells you how wide that mask should be.
Build a mask of k ones where k is the bit-length (treat 0 as length 1), then return n XOR mask.

edge_inputs.description: zero has a single bit, complement is one
all bits set so complement is zero
value with an internal zero bit
wider value with a zero bit
power of two has only its leading bit set

stress.description: random values across the whole allowed range
